free_text: Cross sections for the production of radionuclides by
proton-induced reactions on W, Ta, Pb and Bi from
thresholds up to 2.6 GeV
Center for Radiation Protection and
Radioecology.
Department of Nuclear Chemistry.
Svedberg laboratory
Institute for Particle Physics
Martin Gloris, Dr thesis submitted
to Uni. Hannover
Cross sections for lead target and experimental
details.
Experimental details and analysis of uncertainties.
- data
for V and prelim. data for W.
Metal targets.
The reaction was used
for energy region < 60 MeV.
< 70 MeV
A newly developed procedure was used to improve
the evaluation and calculation of cross sections. Now
the decision whether a nuclide is considered to be
present is based on checking the self-consistency of
all influencing parameters. By supporting the
interpretation of basic quantities used in the
calculations with graphical display systematic errors
are minimized.
A comparison of the experimental data with
theoretical ones calculated by the INC/E models
incorporated into the HERMES and LAHET code systems
and by preequilibrium codes such AREL and ALICE-IPPE
demonstrates the necessity for further improvements
if medium - energy cross sections for applications
shall be reliably predicted by theory.
Total uncertainty includes (see REL-REF):
* Uncertainties due to fluctuations in the beam
intensity become negligible.
Uncertainty of absorption of gamma-quanta
in the sample
* Uncertainty of irradiation time, decay time and
counting time are negligible.
* Uncertainty of impurities - due to the high purity
of the target foils contributions of other constituents
were not considered.
* Uncertainty of nuclide production by secondary
particles
* The dead-time of the detector systems was
automatically corrected
uncertainty of the determination of net
peak areas
uncertainty of half-lives
Uncertainty of gamma-abundances -
abundances were also taken from REL-REF.
Uncertainty of the full-energy-peak efficiency
Uncertainty of number of irradiated nuclei
Uncertainty of flux density
No uncertainties were attributed to the monitor
cross sections.
uncertainty due to gamma-gamma-coincidences
in case of coincident gamma-rays < 5%.
Data tables were sent by R.Michel
Last checking has been done.

N.Otsuka (06.10.2023): ENSDF shows that the gamma lines
coded under DECAY-DATA are not for 172Hf decay but
172gLu decay. Namely, the production cross section of
172Hf was determined by measuring its daughter's
activity.
